chapter_context.summary: Lemuel's mother warns against impurity and strong drink, urging defense for the poor; concludes with the famous alphabetical praise of the Virtuous Woman.

outline.text: Lemuel's Words: Avoiding Wine and Women, Defending the Poor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Lemuel's Words: Avoiding Wine and Women, Defending the Poor (31:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Virtuous Woman: Her Industry and Provisioning Household
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Virtuous Woman: Her Industry and Provisioning Household (31:10-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Her Honor in Gates, Kind Words, and Fearing the LORD
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Her Honor in Gates, Kind Words, and Fearing the LORD (31:23-31), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 31, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
